item.title: George Russell left 'bamboozled' by his drop in pace after good start to F1 season
item.description: The Mercedes driver has not stood on the podium since finishing second in Shanghai in March and finished in sixth on Saturday's qualifying.

item.title: Kimi Antonelli takes statement pole for the Monaco GP
item.description: Kimi Antonelli secured pole position for the 2026 Monaco Grand Prix, beating out Max Verstappen by 0.043-seconds. It will be an...

item.title: F1 drivers always say Monaco is special. But only a hot lap of the track proves it
item.description: The Monaco Grand Prix is a unique driving experience, which in recent years F1 has opened up to luck passengers too

item.title: Ferrari's Fred Vasseur 'under observation' in hospital, misses F1 Monaco Grand Prix qualifying
item.description: The Italian team released a statement on Saturday morning announcing the development.

item.title: F1’s engines will confuse Monaco, too. Plus: Lewis Hamilton leads early
item.description: Welcome to Prime Tire, The Athletic's F1 newsletter.

item.title: Audi wants F1 to stick with current engine power ratio rules in 2027
item.description: Talks over the proposed changes to engine regulations are due to take place this weekend in Monaco.

item.title: McLaren reaches 1,000 F1 races as a reborn giant. The hardest part may still be ahead
item.description: McLaren is celebrating reaching 1000 GPs in 2026. But it's no longer F1's top team and has to overhaul another dominant rival

item.title: F1 Las Vegas Grand Prix extended through 2037 after Clark County approval
item.description: The 10-year deal should unlock infrastructure investment after rocky early years of the race.

item.title: Charles Leclerc's reason for extending with Ferrari is simple: 'I love this team'
item.description: Leclerc also confirmed that he had other options on the table before he signed the extension.

item.title: Monaco GP track breakdown: F1’s crown jewel of risk, precision and rich history
item.description: Fans may dislike the difficulty of overtaking, but the narrow, twisting street track demands the absolute most from drivers.

item.title: Former F1 driver Kevin Magnussen to make NASCAR debut
item.description: The 33-year-old Dane is set to compete in NASCAR’s premier Cup Series in the San Diego area.

item.title: Ferrari renews Charles Leclerc on new multi-year contract
item.description: The 28-year-old has won eight Grands Prix across nine seasons for Ferrari and last renewed his deal in 2024.

item.title: Is this Lewis Hamilton's best chance? Plus: Monaco can be fun this year!
item.description: Welcome to Prime Tire, The Athletic's F1 newsletter.

item.title: Monaco is Lewis Hamilton and Ferrari's best chance yet of beating Mercedes
item.description: On the back of his most successful weekend yet with Ferrari, Lewis Hamilton has a real chance of a first win in Monaco since 2019.

item.title: Mercedes withdraws from talks over Alpine minority stake
item.description: Mercedes has been one of the groups circling the investment opportunity involving Otro Capital's 24 per cent stake.

item.title: SailGP teams share data. Should F1? Plus: New FIA presidential term proposal
item.description: Welcome to Prime Tire, The Athletic's F1 newsletter.

item.title: SailGP makes sure teams share their data. Is the push for transparency working?
item.description: During a typical SailGP weekend, around 80 billion pieces of data is collected, all of which is made available to the teams to analyze.

item.title: Gucci to become Alpine title sponsor from 2027 season
item.description: The Italian fashion company will replace water treatment brand BWT, who has been the F1 team's title sponsor since 2022.

item.title: Inside Lando Norris's Indy 500 visit: Why one sneaky lap may have sold him on racing it
item.description: After a painful retirement in Canada, Norris found an unexpected consolation prize in the race he never planned to fall for.

item.title: F1 mailbag: Does F1 have a competition problem? Why did McLaren make that tire mistake?
item.description: Readers sound off on the latest Formula 1 race in our weekly mailbag.

item.title: What if George Russell had Kimi Antonelli's luck? Plus: Lando Norris' Indy 500 interest
item.description: Welcome to Prime Tire, The Athletic's F1 newsletter.

item.title: I've covered the Monaco GP and Le Mans. Nothing prepared me for the Indy 500
item.description: Our F1 reporter completes motorsport's Triple Crown, and discovers why Indy truly stands alone

item.title: Why Montreal sex workers chose F1's Canadian GP to strike: 'We don't want to pay to work anymore'
item.description: The protest occured during the weekend of the Canadian Grand Prix in Montreal, which had over 360,000 attendees

item.title: Kimi Antonelli is making this F1 title his. George Russell is watching it slip away
item.description: George Russell thinks "the gods don't want me in this fight," and after Canada, it's easy to understand why

item.title: Canadian Grand Prix briefing: Kimi Antonelli wins again as George Russell retires from lead
item.description: The 19-year-old Italian has now won four consecutive races.

item.title: Welcome to F1's new era of 'speed chess,' where every pass comes with a price
item.description: DRS is out. Overtake Mode is in. Here's why drivers say passing just got a lot harder

item.title: Why F1's boiling drama over engine compression ratios matters for the 2026 season
item.description: Has Mercedes found a loophole with its engines? And what exactly is the loophole, anyway?

item.title: F1's new cars behave unlike any others. It's all down to new aerodynamics
item.description: F1 has new cars in 2026, which look different to previous eras in grand prix racing. Here's how they work and what the drivers think

item.title: F1 hits reset in 2026. Your guide to the new cars, new rules and new era
item.description: Formula 1 evolves dramatically in 2026. Here's your year-long guide to it all

item.title: F1's new engines are the sport's biggest gamble in 12 years. Here's what changed
item.description: F1 is introducing new hybrid engines ahead of the 2026 season. Here's how they work and why they matter

item.title: How Formula 1's cost cap works: Rules, penalties and past breaches explained
item.description: The sport's $135 million spending limit aims to create parity — but compliance isn't always simple

item.title: F1 penalty points: How they work and why they're needed even though drivers have doubts
item.description: A deep dive into the details - and arguments - of Formula One penalty points for driver infractions

item.title: What are F1 driver development programs? Inside the academies that nurture future champions
item.description: All of F1's new 2025 drivers have one thing in common - they were part of a team’s driver development program or academy.

item.title: What are F1’s racing rules? A look at race stewards and drivers' battle for clarity
item.description: Multiple times during the 2024 season, drivers have faced questions — and raised their own — about what is and is not allowed on track.

item.title: How F1's Red Bull mastered the art of the 2-second pit stop
item.description: A deep dive into the positions, the practice, and the fine-tuned production that is the Formula One pit stop.

item.title: Welcome to Between the Racing Lines, your new guide to all things F1
item.description: F1 is complicated and confusing at times, and is constantly evolving. Here's your guide to keeping up.

item.title: Sim time, data crunching and Candy Crush: The life of an F1 reserve driver
item.description: Reserve drivers are more than simple substitutes — they play important roles within their teams, even if they rarely get to hit the track.

item.title: How F1 drivers train physically and mentally to go from 'gym fit' to 'race fit'
item.description: To have a chance at the title, drivers need not just a top-caliber car but to extract the maximum performance from it — and themselves.
